summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the vulnerable script execution endpoint allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript code on the server. The exploit creates a proof file on the server filesystem, demonstrating successful remote code execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the malicious JavaScript payload to be executed on the server, creating a proof file at /tmp/pwned.txt without requiring authentication. This demonstrates that an unauthenticated remote attacker can achieve arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully built and launched FUXA, sent a malicious script to the /api/runscript endpoint without authentication, and the script was executed on the server. The proof file /tmp/pwned.txt was created, confirming that arbitrary JavaScript code execution was achieved through the vulnerable script engine.
verification_method: The exploit script executes JavaScript code that creates a proof file at /tmp/pwned.txt containing the hostname and timestamp. The verification script checks for the existence and content of this file. The presence of this file confirms that attacker-controlled code was executed on the server.
evidence_produced_by_run: The malicious script was accepted and executed by the /api/runscript endpoint without authentication.
A proof file was successfully created at /tmp/pwned.txt on the server.
The proof file contains the server hostname and timestamp, confirming code execution in the server context.
No authentication token was required to execute the malicious script, confirming the default configuration has no authentication.
The script execution engine's Module._compile() function successfully executed arbitrary JavaScript code.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the FUXA repository from GitHub on the host, checks out the pinned version tag v1.2.9-2654, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
